title: Preventing Middleware-Induced Route Conflicts in Express

statement.markdown: Write a bug-fix for the given Express middleware code to prevent route conflicts. Given that the code has a logical error that causes routes to conflict, your task is to identify and fix the issue so that routes are handled correctly without conflicts.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an HTTP request object.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be an HTTP response object.
statement.notes: The code is designed to handle GET and POST requests to the root URL and the /users URL.

examples.output:

Content-Type: text/plain

Hello, Express!

examples.explanation: Example GET request to the /users URL

hints.text: Review the provided middleware code for potential route conflicts.

editorial.title: Understanding Route Conflicts in Express
editorial.intuition: Route conflicts occur when multiple route handlers are applied to the same route, causing unexpected behavior. To fix this issue, prioritize route handlers or adjust the middleware order to ensure routes are handled correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute-force solution involves listing all possible routes and handlers to identify conflicts.
editorial.optimalSolution: Prioritize the order of route handlers or adjust middleware order to prevent conflicts.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof involves demonstrating that the optimal solution prevents route conflicts.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run involves creating a dry-run environment to test the corrected middleware code.

functionSignature.javascript:
var express = require('express');
var app = express();
